Identity.History: As the first settlers of Aotearoa New Zealand and the guardians of a rich oral tradition, the Māori were famed as masters of carving, weaving and the powerful performance art of haka. The Māori were also formidable warriors, with a deeply ingrained culture of combat and honour. At their peak, the great iwi (tribes) commanded vast territories across both islands, bound together by whakapapa — a profound system of genealogy that connected people to their ancestors, the land, and the gods.
